overview.market_size: US industrial services market estimated at $50-70B (includes industrial cleaning, tank cleaning, turnaround services, scaffolding, insulation)
overview.growth_rate: 4-6% CAGR driven by refinery maintenance, infrastructure spending, and environmental compliance
overview.fragmentation: Highly fragmented — mix of large national players and thousands of regional specialists
overview.typical_ebitda_margin: 12%–20% depending on specialization; hazmat and specialty services at higher end
overview.recurring_revenue_note: Master Service Agreements (MSAs) with refineries, chemical plants, and manufacturers create recurring revenue base. Turnaround/shutdown work is cyclical but predictable.
overview.pe_interest: Strong — industrial services is a well-established PE consolidation thesis. Multiple active platforms.
overview.labor_challenge: Significant — specialized trade certifications, safety training requirements, aging workforce
overview.ai_disruption_risk: Low — physical industrial services, confined space work, and hazmat handling resistant to automation

$2M-$5M.source: Industry estimates / industrial services M&A data
$2M-$5M.notes: Specialized industrial services (tank cleaning, hydro blasting, coatings) command premium over generalist contractors.

$5M-$10M.notes: MSA portfolios with blue-chip industrial clients support higher multiples.

$10M-$15M.notes: Multi-service, multi-site industrial services platforms with established MSAs and safety records.

$15M-$25M.notes: Large industrial services companies (Clean Harbors, GFL) trade at 10-14x. Strong sector with proven PE playbook.

value_drivers.explanation: Long-term MSAs with refineries, chemical plants, and manufacturers provide revenue predictability and reduce sales cost.
Low Experience Modification Rate (EMR) and Total Recordable Incident Rate (TRIR) are table stakes for major industrial clients. Poor safety = deal killer.
Confined space, hazmat, radiation, and specialty welding certifications create barriers to entry.
Companies offering multiple services (cleaning + inspection + maintenance) become one-stop shops for industrial clients.
Industrial services companies often have concentrated client bases. Diversification across plants/sites mitigates risk.
Companies handling waste manifesting, disposal, and environmental reporting add compliance value.
Turnaround work is cyclical but predictable (refineries schedule years ahead). Established turnaround relationships are valuable.

recent_transactions.rationale: Industrial services consolidation continuing as PE platforms add geographic and service capabilities.

recent_transactions.target: Regional industrial cleaning and specialty services companies
recent_transactions.acquirer: Clean Harbors, GFL Environmental, Heritage Environmental
recent_transactions.rationale: Large strategics continuously acquiring to expand service footprint and client relationships.

pe_platforms.name: Various PE firms
pe_platforms.thesis: Consolidation of fragmented industrial services providers to build regional/national platforms with diverse service offerings and MSA portfolios
